title: oxalis spiralis subsp. trichophora
intro: Oxalis spiralis subsp. trichophora, also known as the "creeping wood-sorrel," is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Oxalidaceae family. It is a perennial plant with heart-shaped leaves and small, white flowers, native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and has been used to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its health benefits and medicinal properties.
